observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal GSO to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan
(PIP) to the Audit Team at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory
taking activities in adopting COA Circular No. 2020-006 relative to the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts; and
b. Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to make a reconciliation for the
noted variance in the recorded PPE as against the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
verify and confirm with the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of
P5,552,712.53 and P388,073.93 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal
accounts reflected in the books of the Province and adjust the same to present
the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and
the Business Permits and Licensing Officer to require the business owners to
submit the sworn declaration of gross sales or receipts, audited FS, or a certified
copy of the ITR.

We reiterated our
recommendation that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the

MGSO to make a complete
reconciliation for the noted
difference in the recorded PPE
as against the RPCPPE and
constantly monitor the progress
of the reconciliation. We also
recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the
accountable officer to pay the
cost of missing PPE amounting
to P23,249.40.
observations.managementAction: Discrepancy was significantly reduced through the continuing reconciliation, however it was not completed. The matter was discussed with the accountable officer. It will be deducted on the terminal leave.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work and the pandemic situation, the reconciliation was not finished in due time.

Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to make a complete
reconciliation for the noted difference in the recorded PPE as against the RPCPPE. We
also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the accountable officer to pay the
cost of missing PPE amounting to P30,249.40.

remedies regarding collections of delinquent taxes provided for under Sections 256
and 257 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to furnish the Municipal

We further recommended that the appraisal committee make a
report/recommendation of the fair and reasonable appraisal of all donated
properties to facilitate the adjustment in the books of accounts, for fair
presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
monitor the immediate liquidation of cash advances of all concerned accountable
officers and employees whenever the purpose for which it was given has been
served and see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year for
fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General
Services Officer (MGSO) to submit the IIRUP with complete documentation based
on the results of the physical inventory in order to facilitate the disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees of their claims and request
them to submit supporting documents to the Municipality. In the event that they
fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of
the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a
Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that
claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct and complete the physical count of the Municipality’s
property and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Issue an office order creating the Appraisal Committee who will appraise the
PPE which has no available information regarding its acquisition date and
cost;
b) Constantly monitor the status of physical inventory and appraisal of PPE to
ensure completion and submission of complete RPCPPE within the set
deadline;
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the
Schedule of recorded PPE;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant and MGSO to make a reconciliation for the
noted differences in the recorded PPE and RPCPPE;
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the request for
relief from accountability with approval by the COA-CP for the dropped PPE
totaling P1,241,778.82; and
f) Require the accountable officer to pay the cost of PPE which were found
missing/stolen totaling P16,000.00.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) ICO-MT to prepare the list of individual taxpayers with delinquencies for CYs
2002 to 2018 as of December 31, 2018 and furnish it to the Municipal
Accountant;
b) ICO-MT to prepare a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-
collection or non-payment and submit it to the Sangguniang Bayan together
with the list of taxpayers with delinquencies with a request for assistance in
the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160 in
order for the Municipality to have funds for other projects that will benefit its
constituents; and
c) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments of RPT/SET
Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers is received from the MT to
reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to furnish the Municipal
Accountant the copy of Deed of Sale for one unrecorded purchased lot in
order for the latter to adjust the books of accounts; and
b) Municipal Accountant to make an adjustment for the erroneous recording of
one parcel with Title Number 135951 amounting to P1,225,000.00.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) MGSO to secure from the Registry of Deeds the owner’s copy of title for the
remaining two parcels of land; and
b) OIC-Municipal Assessor and MGSO to facilitate the titling of the 18 parcels of
land in the name of the Municipality.
We further reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor create an
appraisal committee/team to properly value all donated land as to their fair values
at the time of acquisition to facilitate adjustment in the books of accounts, for fair
presentation of Land and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-MT to return to the
Accounting Office the ten unclaimed/undelivered checks together with the DVs
and supporting documents to facilitate proper adjustment in the books of
accounts.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) ICO-MT and Municipal Accountant to verify the existence and validity of the
claims under the said 45 checks;
b) Municipal Accountant to make proper adjustments in the books of accounts
by debiting the Cash in Bank account and crediting either the Accounts
Payable, Prior Period Adjustments or the pertinent trust liability accounts
once the validation of claims is complete, for proper and fair presentation of
cash and other affected accounts in the financial statements; and
c) ICO-MT to facilitate the replacement of the checks for valid claims and
immediately deliver/remit the same to the payees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustment for the over recording of
liquidation in the amount of P25,000.00 by debiting and crediting the Advances to
Special Disbursing Officer account and Prior Period Adjustments account,
respectively.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor submit to
ICFAU the liquidation report for his CA for CF of P105,000.00 without further delay
and thereafter furnish the Audit Team with a copy of relative transmittal letter duly
stamped received by ICFAU.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate strict compliance with
the cited regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the Notes to FS include disclosures for material
differences between the final budget and actual amounts in order for the users to
properly understand the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-DTTB-DOH to secure
authority from the Head of the National Center for Pharmaceutical Access and
Management for the purchase of medicines that are not included in the PNF;
otherwise the total amount of P154,380.00 will be disallowed in audit.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all
purchases of drugs and medicines are made by the Rural Health Unit and not the
Municipal Social Worker and Development Officer or any other offices, the
former being the proper authority and expert on the field.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC/
DTTB-DOH to ensure that the medicines to be procured are included in the lists
provided by the PNF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the ICO-MT a service vehicle
that the latter could use in depositing his collections promptly and intact, thereby
avoiding therisk of loss and/or misuse of undeposited collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant and
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to submit the lacking documents to
the Audit Team for evaluation.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 in inviting an
observer to enhance the transparency during procurement activities of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of PPE;
